scenario: A FEEL expression computes a numeric value 12.7 and needs to apply different rounding strategies: round down to 12, round up to 13, round to nearest 13. The team wants to know FEEL's rounding functions.
question: Which FEEL functions provide floor, ceiling, and round?

options.text: md:29-46), ceiling(n) rounds up toward positive infinity (:66-83), and FEEL provides 4 named rounding variants with scale: round up(n, scale) (:103-127), round down(n, scale) (:129-152), round half up(n, scale) (:155-178), round half down(n, scale) (:181-204). NOTE: there is no round half even (banker's rounding) built-in in FEEL -- that semantics must be implemented via custom FEEL or by using round half up/round half down based on tie direction.

FEEL provides multiple rounding functions beyond a single generic round(). The documented numeric built-ins include floor(n), ceiling(n), round up(n, scale), round down(n, scale), round half up(n, scale), and round half down(n, scale). Each serves a distinct rounding strategy. Claiming only one rounding function exists misrepresents the breadth of FEEL's numeric built-ins and would lead developers to use incorrect rounding behaviour for their specific financial or mathematical requirements.

round() are JavaScript syntax patterns that do not work in FEEL. FEEL does not use object-qualified method calls with dot notation. 5, 0). Developers familiar with JavaScript must adapt to FEEL's functional syntax.

FEEL provides comprehensive rounding support through its numeric built-in functions. The floor, ceiling, round up, round down, round half up, and round half down functions are all documented in the FEEL numeric built-ins reference. These cover all standard rounding strategies needed for financial calculations, data normalization, and general arithmetic. The claim that rounding lacks support is factually incorrect.

a.text: Correct. FEEL's numeric built-ins per feel-built-in-functions-numeric.md provide these rounding variants (verified verbatim from :29-204): floor(n) (:29-46) / floor(n, scale) (:49-63): rounds toward negative infinity. floor(12.7) = 12; floor(-12.3) = -13. ceiling(n) (:66-83) / ceiling(n, scale) (:86-100): rounds toward positive infinity. ceiling(12.3) = 13; ceiling(-12.7) = -12. round up(n, scale) (:103-127): rounds away from zero. round up(5.5) = 6, round up(-5.5) = -6. round down(n, scale) (:129-152): rounds toward zero. round down(5.5, 0) = 5. round half up(n, scale) (:155-178): rounds half-up (school rounding). round half up(5.5, 0) = 6, round half up(-5.5, 0) = -6. round half down(n, scale) (:181-204): rounds half-down. round half down(5.5, 0) = 5. No round half even / banker's rounding built-in exists in FEEL -- grep for round half even across docs/components/modeler/feel/ returns 0 hits. If banker's rounding is required (financial calculations), it must be implemented via custom FEEL logic or by selecting round half up/round half down per tie direction. Choose based on requirement: floor/ceiling for "always toward one side"; round-half-up for everyday "round to nearest" (standard school rounding); round-up/round-down for unconditional direction; decimal(n, scale) (per feel-built-in-functions-conversion.md) rounds to a specific decimal scale.

c.text: Incorrect. FEEL uses bare function names.

No round half even / banker's rounding built-in exists in FEEL -- grep for round half even across docs/components/modeler/feel/ returns 0 hits. If banker's rounding is required (financial calculations), it must be implemented via custom FEEL logic or by selecting round half up/round half down per tie direction.
